summary: PR 2601 links Kirby's Sheik-copy special-N translation unit by shrinking `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` to the 0x80105FEC-0x80106F44 range, marking it Matching, and moving the following Mewtwo-copy range into a new nonmatching `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c/.h` split. It also updates split/symbol metadata, introduces a shared Kirby special-N callback inline, renames duplicate local callback-setter inlines from `ftKirbyDmgInline` to `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`, and adjusts includes for the new Mewtwo-copy header.

key_files.role: Changed Kirby split ranges: shortened `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`, added `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c`, and moved rodata/sdata2 ownership ranges.
Adjusted data and sdata2 symbol names/scopes around Kirby special split boundaries.
Build status change: `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` becomes Matching and new `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` is added as NonMatching.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:configure.py Object(NonMatching ftkirbyspecialseak.c) -> Object(Matching ftkirbyspecialseak.c), plus Object(NonMatching ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c)

key_files.role: Main linked source file, reduced by 1192 lines and updated to include shared Kirby inlines.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftkirbyspecialseak.c adds #include "inlines.h" and removes several includes

key_files.role: New nonmatching split holding Mewtwo-copy Kirby special-N code formerly in the larger range.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c new file includes ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.h, inlines.h, and itmewtwoshadowball.h

key_files.role: New header for Mewtwo-copy Kirby special-N declarations.
New shared Kirby inline header defining `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:inlines.h new file with static inline ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs setting death2_cb and take_dmg_cb

key_files.role: Adds include for the new Mewtwo-copy Kirby special header.
Renames local damage callback inlines and their call sites to `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs` / `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs2`.
Renames duplicate local callback inline and call sites for Link-copy Kirby special-N.
Renames duplicate local callback inline and call sites for Samus-copy Kirby special-N.
Renames an inline callback setter from `ftKirbyDmgInline` to `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`.
Removes two extern Vec3 declarations after rodata split movement.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftkirbyspecialzelda.c removes extern Vec3 const ftKb_Init_803B7570 and ftKb_Init_803B757C

key_files.role: Removes a block of declarations no longer owned by the Donkey-copy header.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h hunk removes declarations after ftKb_SkSpecialAirNCancel_Coll

key_files.role: Updates include from Donkey-copy Kirby header to the new Mewtwo-copy Kirby header.

what_changed: Marked `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialseak.c` Matching in `configure.py` and introduced `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` as NonMatching.
Split the prior `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` address range so Sheik-copy code ends at 0x80106F44 and Mewtwo-copy code begins at 0x80106F44.
Moved data/rodata/sdata2 ownership in `splits.txt` around `ftKb_Init.c`, `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`, and new `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c`.
Converted several global/address-style data symbols near 0x803CB794 and 0x804D94F8 to local `@...` symbols in `symbols.txt`.
Added `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c/.h` with Mewtwo-copy Kirby special-N declarations and implementation body.
Added `ftKirby/inlines.h` containing `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`, then reused or aligned callback-setter naming across several Kirby copied-special files.
Adjusted includes so `ftKb_Init.c` and `itmewtwoshadowball.c` reference the new Mewtwo-copy Kirby header.
smart_moves: The PR isolates a matching prefix of a previously larger nonmatching TU instead of trying to land the entire old `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` range at once.
It keeps the unmatched successor range reviewable by adding `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` as NonMatching rather than dropping or hiding the remaining code.
It updates `splits.txt`, `symbols.txt`, and `configure.py` together, which makes the code-status change consistent with section ownership changes visible in the diff.
It replaces vague local helper names like `ftKirbyDmgInline` with a callback-oriented name, `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`, matching the observed side effects on `death2_cb` and `take_dmg_cb`.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matching opportunity covers only the first part of a previously monolithic nonmatching TU, a narrow split can link that prefix while preserving the following range as a new NonMatching object.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:splits.txt ftkirbyspecialseak text end changed to 0x80106F44
diff_excerpt:configure.py ftkirbyspecialseak.c Matching and ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c NonMatching

decomp_lessons.lesson: A source split may require adjacent rodata/data/sdata2 ownership and symbol-scope updates, not just a new C file.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:splits.txt moves ftKb_Init rodata end to 0x803B7570 and assigns ftkirbyspecialmewtwo rodata 0x803B7570-0x803B7588
diff_excerpt:symbols.txt changes data/sdata2 symbols from ftKb_Init_* globals to local @ labels

decomp_lessons.lesson: Repeated small inlines that set the same fighter callbacks can be made more reviewable by naming them for the actual side effect, when the body clearly supports that name.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:ftKb_SpecialN.c body sets death2_cb and take_dmg_cb
diff_excerpt:ftKb_SpecialNLk.c and ftKb_SpecialNSs.c rename ftKirbyDmgInline to ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs

naming_conventions.name: ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs
naming_conventions.meaning: Inline helper that sets Kirby special-N-related fighter callbacks, specifically `death2_cb` and `take_dmg_cb` in the shown body.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:inlines.h body assigns fp->death2_cb and fp->take_dmg_cb

naming_conventions.name: ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs2
naming_conventions.meaning: Local inline helper variant that sets only `take_dmg_cb` in `ftKb_SpecialN.c`.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:ftKb_SpecialN.c renamed ftKirbyDmgInline2 to ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs2 and body assigns fp->take_dmg_cb

naming_conventions.name: ftkirbyspecialmewtwo
naming_conventions.meaning: New file/header naming follows the existing Kirby copied-special file pattern and houses Mewtwo-copy special-N declarations/implementation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Link a smaller address range by cutting the old TU at an exact function/object boundary and placing the remaining code in a new nonmatching object.
Update symbol scope/name metadata to reflect local ownership after section split changes instead of compensating in C source.
Move reusable inline behavior into a Kirby-local inline header when a newly split file also needs the helper.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that link `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` after PR 2601.
Search for remaining `ftKirbyDmgInline` names after this PR to see whether the callback helper naming was normalized elsewhere.
Search PRs touching `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs` or `ftKirby/inlines.h` for follow-up cleanup or header ownership changes.
Check later changes to symbols around `ftKb_Init_803CB794`, `@304`, `@352`, `@249`, `@257`, and `@480` for data ownership corrections.
Search for other PRs that split a nonmatching fighter TU into a matching prefix plus a new nonmatching successor object.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Link a matching prefix by splitting off the remaining nonmatching successor range
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2601 changed `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` from one NonMatching object covering 0x80105FEC-0x80109290 into a Matching object ending at 0x80106F44 plus a new NonMatching `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` starting at 0x80106F44.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Name helper inlines for callback side effects when the body proves the role
accepted_candidate_records.text: The diff renames `ftKirbyDmgInline` to `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`; the shown helper body assigns `death2_cb` and `take_dmg_cb`, making the new name more descriptive than a generic damage-inline name.

source_update_candidates.title: Focused TU split for partial matching progress
source_update_candidates.text: When only a prefix or subrange of a nonmatching TU is ready to link, consider splitting the TU at verified section/function boundaries, marking the linked range Matching and preserving the remaining range as a new NonMatching object, with corresponding splits/symbol metadata updates.
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR provides a concrete example of linking `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` by isolating the still-nonmatching Mewtwo-copy range.

source_update_candidates.title: Kirby copied special-N Mewtwo split path introduced
source_update_candidates.text: `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` and `.h` were introduced as the Mewtwo-copy Kirby special-N split adjacent to `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`; `configure.py` initially lists the new C file as NonMatching in this PR.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path ownership fact for future curator review; evidence is limited to this PR's diff and should be checked against current source before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` matched in this PR; `configure.py` explicitly adds it as NonMatching.
Do not claim objdiff or regression validation from this slice; no such logs are loaded.

Do not generalize the local `@...` symbol changes into a universal rule without current symbol/source verification.
